name: OC San
small_business_notes: Register at least one alternate contact; keep commodity codes and DIR registration details current.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Engineering - Instrumentation & Controls; Electrical/Electronic equipment; monitoring equipment; water/wastewater chemicals; SCADA/controls-related services
scan_priority_reason: SCADA-heavy wastewater agency with public works, design/A&E, goods, and services contacts published.
next_setup_action: Register/subscribe in OC San OpenGov; add alternate email; add controls/instrumentation commodity categories; email purchasing@ocsan.gov for goods/services fit.
notes: Prior source inventory listed PlanetBids; current official page says OpenGov. Verify portal during account setup.

name: Orange County Water District
recommended_codes_or_categories: Engineering Services; Contractor Electrical; Computer Related Hardware and Software; Scientific Equipment and Supplies; Software/IT; Utilities; Waste Management and Remediation; NAICS/UNSPSC for controls/instrumentation
scan_priority_reason: Groundwater Replenishment System and treatment/lab operations create controls, instrumentation, LIMS, and equipment opportunities.
next_setup_action: Complete OCWD vendor registration form; include NAICS/UNSPSC and controls/software/services categories; monitor RFP/contracts page; verify best procurement contact via procurement@ocwd.com.
notes: Contact name came from recent RFP documents; use procurement@ocwd.com as general channel.

name: IRWD
small_business_notes: New suppliers complete Supplier Assessment Information Sheet, Business License, and W-9 before ePurchasing registration.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Engineering; electrical; instrumentation and controls; telemetry; SCADA support; water/wastewater treatment services; software/IT
scan_priority_reason: Large water district with vendor pre-qualification and PlanetBids access; strong fit for treatment, telemetry, and controls.
next_setup_action: Submit Supplier Assessment Information Sheet, business license, and W-9 to purchasingdept@irwd.com; then register in ePurchasing/PlanetBids and join IRWD Connect.
notes: IRWD Connect also lists CommunityRelations@IRWD.com for program questions.

name: County of Orange / OC Public Works
procurement_contact_name: Daisy Corona; Jennifer Mason; Albert Rodriguez
local_preference: OC Local Small Business Preference; DVBE Preference
small_business_notes: Register in OpenGov as County supplier; OC Local Small Business and DVBE preference policies are referenced by OCPW.
recommended_codes_or_categories: UNSPSC: electrical systems/services; industrial control systems; engineering services; public works; water/sewer infrastructure; telemetry/communications
scan_priority_reason: County infrastructure and OC Public Works solicitations can surface electrical distribution, facilities controls, and water/sewer projects.
next_setup_action: Register in County OpenGov; subscribe to OCPW categories; review OC Local Small Business/DVBE eligibility; monitor public works and facility electrical projects.
notes: OCPW page also references legacy BidSync award review; OpenGov is the current portal shown.

name: City of Irvine
small_business_notes: DBE/SBE resources referenced; local vendor preference requires Irvine physical location and business license.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Category codes for consulting services; construction services general; electrical; controls; IT/software; facilities maintenance
scan_priority_reason: Local city procurement with 5% local preference and online bidding; opportunities include public works, facilities, utilities, and controls-adjacent work.
next_setup_action: Register in City Bids Online/PlanetBids; add all relevant category codes; evaluate local vendor preference eligibility; contact Purchasing Team for category guidance.
notes: Administrative Services Department phone on FAQ is 949-724-6255; vendor page lists Purchasing Team at 949-724-6180.

name: City of Anaheim
local_preference: 1% pricing allowance for Anaheim material suppliers based on sales-tax return
small_business_notes: Registration requires alternate email, applicable licenses, NIGP category codes, primary contact, and Tax ID/SSN.
recommended_codes_or_categories: NIGP codes for electrical supplies/services; instrumentation; controls; SCADA; water/wastewater equipment; software/IT; facilities maintenance
scan_priority_reason: High-volume city procurement; formal solicitations $50k+ and OpenGov notifications by NIGP category.
next_setup_action: Register in Anaheim OpenGov; add NIGP codes; add alternate email; confirm Anaheim business license requirements if awarded.
notes: Email decoded from official protected link; verify in portal before sending.

name: City of Santa Ana
small_business_notes: Purchasing Division asks vendors to schedule appointments; initial contact with departments should go through Purchasing.
recommended_codes_or_categories: PlanetBids categories for public works, water and sewer services, traffic signals, electrical, instrumentation, controls, software/IT
scan_priority_reason: City has water/sewer services, traffic signals, and public works bids in PlanetBids; good controls/field-services adjacency.
next_setup_action: Register in PlanetBids portal 20137; subscribe to public works, water/sewer, traffic signal, and electrical categories; contact Buyers@santa-ana.org for vendor setup questions.
notes: Public Works page confirms PlanetBids for all bids/proposals.

name: Santa Margarita Water District
small_business_notes: Registration does not guarantee award; District may request insurance, qualifications, and experience details.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Water/wastewater electrical; instrumentation; monitoring; SCADA; telemetry; engineering; maintenance services
scan_priority_reason: South OC water district with dedicated PlanetBids portal and direct purchasing contact; strong water automation fit.
next_setup_action: Register in PlanetBids portal 75207; contact purchasing@smwd.com for process questions; add controls/instrumentation and water/wastewater service categories.
notes: AP invoice contact is separate and not procurement lead.

name: EMWD
small_business_notes: All formal solicitations are through Euna Procurement; vendors must keep contact/address information current.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Water/wastewater instrumentation; electrical; telemetry; SCADA; process controls; pumps/motors; software/IT; engineering services
scan_priority_reason: Large regional water/wastewater district with formal online bid management and strong operational controls needs.
next_setup_action: Register in Euna Procurement; add product/service categories; contact Kellie Gomez for general questions; monitor Engineering public works and Purchasing goods/services paths.
notes: Some older EMWD pages mention Bonfire/Myrna Vazquez; current official page says Euna/Kellie Gomez.

name: IEUA
small_business_notes: IEUA uses PlanetBids for bids, RFPs, RFQs, addenda, and bid results; vendors register via Vendor Registration link.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Water/wastewater engineering; electrical; instrumentation and controls; SCADA; recycled water; treatment operations; software/IT
scan_priority_reason: Inland Empire utilities agency with treatment and recycled-water infrastructure; likely controls and instrumentation demand.
next_setup_action: Open IEUA Doing Business page and PlanetBids Vendor Registration; subscribe to electrical, instrumentation/controls, and treatment categories; separately verify direct purchasing contact.
notes: Official page did not expose a direct procurement email/phone in search output; verify before outreach.

name: LADWP
local_preference: SBE/DBE/LBE self-certification in eRSP; cyber vendor prequalification may apply
small_business_notes: Vendors providing protected cyber assets/services may need CIP-013 SCRM evaluation via VendorCyberRisk@ladwp.com before/alongside eRSP registration.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Cyber assets; SCADA; protected cyber assets; serial gateways; modems; relay protection devices; IDS/IPS; electrical controls; software/IT; communications
scan_priority_reason: Huge water/power utility; controls, cyber, relay, SCADA, and instrumentation opportunities but setup burden is higher.
next_setup_action: Register/reactivate in eRSP; also register in RAMPLA for listed opportunities; if bidding cyber/control assets, email VendorCyberRisk@ladwp.com for CIP-013 SCRM process.
notes: General page redacted one email in search output; eRSP guide gives exact help desk email/phone.

name: Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
local_preference: 5% reduction credit for small/DVBE; additional 5% for regional firms on procurement contracts
small_business_notes: Goal of 25% outside contracts to small/regional/DVBE businesses; certifications can matter for incentives.
recommended_codes_or_categories: Electrical equipment; computer software; pipes/valves; water treatment chemicals; engineering; communications networking; construction; treatment systems
scan_priority_reason: Regional water distribution, power generation, treatment systems, and Pure Water-related programs create high-value controls/automation opportunities.
next_setup_action: Register in Metropolitan eProcurement/NETConnect; review Business Outreach programs; contact Procurement/Purchasing Services and Business Outreach for certification/incentive fit.
notes: Business Outreach contacts include John Arena (213) 217-5519 and certification officers; use for small/regional positioning.

name: County of San Diego
local_preference: 15% local preference up to $150k for qualifying local certified DVBE/Veteran/Small Businesses
small_business_notes: Register with UNSPSC commodity codes; automated notices should not be relied on exclusively; check BuyNet regularly.
recommended_codes_or_categories: UNSPSC for industrial control systems; electrical services; water/sewer services; telemetry; software/IT; public works; facilities systems
scan_priority_reason: Large county procurement; less water-specialized than districts but has public works/facility controls opportunities.
next_setup_action: Register in BuyNet; add UNSPSC commodities; review small-local preference and certifications; set recurring manual scan for controls/water/facilities solicitations.
notes: County explicitly says viewing solicitations does not require registration but submitting responses does.

name: City of San Diego
small_business_notes: Vendor portal covers suppliers, contractors, subcontractors, consultants, manufacturers, distributors, and dealers.
recommended_codes_or_categories: PlanetBids categories for electrical; control systems; SCADA; telemetry; water/wastewater; software/IT; construction services; consultant services
scan_priority_reason: Large city with formal PlanetBids portal and partner agencies; good scan target for municipal controls and water infrastructure.
next_setup_action: Register in City PlanetBids portal; add controls/water/electrical categories; use purchasing@sandiego.gov for portal content questions; set 24-48h scans.
notes: Technical support is separate from procurement-content questions.

name: Cal eProcure / California DGS
procurement_contact_name: Office of Small Business and DVBE Services
technical_support_contact: DGS eProcurement: eprocure@dgs.ca.gov / (916) 375-2000; DGS procurement: custserv@dgs.ca.gov / 1-800-559-5529
local_preference: 5% SB bid preference where applicable; DVBE participation programs
small_business_notes: Apply for SB/DVBE certification; maintain UNSPSC and keywords; FI$Cal vendor management may require STD 204 for state payments.
recommended_codes_or_categories: UNSPSC for PLC/SCADA, control systems, electrical equipment/services, instrumentation, software/IT, water treatment equipment, engineering services
scan_priority_reason: Broad state portal; useful for DWR/Caltrans/Water Boards and state facilities, but broad scanning needs filters.
next_setup_action: Register business in Cal eProcure; add UNSPSC/keywords; apply for SB/DVBE if eligible; configure saved searches for water, controls, SCADA, instrumentation, DWR, Water Boards, and Caltrans.
notes: Separate vendor-management support also exists through FI$Cal.

name: SAM.gov
technical_support_contact: FSD via https://www.fsd.gov; live help Mon-Fri 8am-8pm ET
local_preference: Small-business programs via SBA / federal socioeconomic categories
small_business_notes: SAM.gov is free; entity registration creates/uses UEI; registration must be renewed every 365 days. APEX Accelerators can help for free.
recommended_codes_or_categories: NAICS 541330; 541512; 238210; 334513; 334512; PSC J059/J066/7A20-style IT/maintenance as applicable; keywords PLC, SCADA, controls, instrumentation, telemetry, water treatment
scan_priority_reason: Federal opportunities can be high-value but broad and competitive; best for filtered saved searches and subcontracting intelligence.
next_setup_action: Set up Login.gov/SAM.gov; register entity or get UEI; configure saved searches for NAICS/keywords; contact APEX Accelerator if registration help is needed.
notes: Do not pay third-party SAM registration vendors; official registration/UEI is free.
